transformation_type: arylamine plus beta-dicarbonyl annulation to quinolines / 4-hydroxyquinolines
mechanism_type: acid-catalyzed Schiff base formation, enamine isomerization, cyclization, and dehydration
common_conditions: acid/heat-promoted condensation and cyclodehydration of aromatic amines with beta-dicarbonyl compounds
description_short: Acid-catalyzed condensation of aryl amines with beta-dicarbonyl compounds to furnish substituted quinolines or 4-hydroxyquinolines through Schiff base intermediates.

transformation_type: tertiary amine N-oxide syn elimination to alkene and hydroxylamine
mechanism_type: stereospecific thermal syn elimination via five-membered cyclic transition state
common_conditions: oxidize tertiary amine to the N-oxide, then heat to induce syn beta-elimination
description_short: Thermal syn elimination of tertiary amine N-oxides to alkenes and dialkylhydroxylamines through a cyclic transition state.

transformation_type: 1,5-diene [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement / ring expansion of strained dienes
mechanism_type: concerted thermal [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement through chairlike or boatlike transition states
common_conditions: thermal rearrangement of 1,5-dienes, often accelerated by strain release or product stabilization
description_short: Thermal reversible [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement of 1,5-dienes that reorganizes carbon skeletons, including medium-ring-forming strained-substrate variants.

transformation_type: enantioselective ketone reduction to secondary alcohols
mechanism_type: oxazaborolidine-catalyzed borane hydride transfer to the carbonyl face-selectively
common_conditions: chiral oxazaborolidine catalyst with borane source, commonly at low temperature
description_short: Catalytic enantioselective reduction of ketones to secondary alcohols using chiral oxazaborolidine catalysts and borane reagents.

transformation_type: sulfur ylide methylene transfer to carbonyls or enones forming epoxides or cyclopropanes
mechanism_type: nucleophilic addition of sulfoxonium or sulfonium ylides followed by intramolecular displacement
common_conditions: base generation of sulfur ylides followed by reaction with aldehydes, ketones, or alpha,beta-unsaturated carbonyl compounds
description_short: Sulfur ylide transfer chemistry that converts carbonyl compounds into epoxides and enones into cyclopropanes under basic conditions.
